(2) Write 3-5 Key Results that are measurable, outcome-based (not task lists), and time-bound, each with a baseline and a target number. (3) Flag any KR that is actually a task or a vanity metric and rewrite it. (4) Suggest 2-3 weekly leading indicators I can check to know if I'm on track before quarter-end. (5) Score the final set 1-10 on ambition and measurability and say what would make it a 10.
OUTPUT FORMAT: the Objective on its own line, then KRs as a table (KR | Baseline | Target | How measured), then leading indicators, then the score. Push me to be ambitious but honest; if my ambition is too vague to measure, say so and propose a sharper version.
h: Describe what you or your team want to achieve this quarter in [AMBITION].

p:
You are a neutral decision advisor trained in decision science. My decision: [DECISION]. Options I'm weighing: [OPTIONS]. This is general thinking support, not professional financial, legal, or medical advice.
Walk me through this: (1) Propose the 3 values/criteria that should matter most here and ask me to confirm or edit them. (2) Build a weighted decision matrix scoring each option against those criteria. (3) Run a pre-mortem on the leading option: top 3 ways it could fail and an early warning sign for each. (4) Apply a reversibility test - is this a one-way or two-way door? (5) Give a clear recommendation with a confidence level and the single biggest assumption it rests on.
OUTPUT FORMAT: numbered sections as above, with the matrix as a table (Option | each criterion | Weighted total). Be the advisor who tells me what I need to hear, not what I want to hear.
h: State the decision you're stuck on in [DECISION] and any options in [OPTIONS].

p:
Act as a knowledge-management expert who has set up second-brain systems for busy professionals. My note-taking tool: [TOOL]. My current projects and areas of responsibility: [PROJECTS].
Design a complete second-brain system: (1) A PARA structure (Projects, Areas, Resources, Archive) populated with MY actual projects and areas. (2) A capture workflow - exactly where to put fleeting notes, articles, and ideas so nothing gets lost. (3) Three simple tagging/naming conventions that scale. (4) A 'progressive summarization' rule so notes stay useful months later. (5) A 15-minute weekly review checklist to keep it from rotting.
OUTPUT FORMAT: a numbered setup guide plus a visual folder map (indented tree). Favor simple and sustainable over clever and complex; if my tool can't do something cleanly, suggest the closest native workaround.
h: Name your note app in [TOOL] and your main projects/roles in [PROJECTS].

p:
You are a time-management analyst who finds hidden patterns people can't see in their own schedules. Here is a typical week of my calendar/time log:
<calendar>
[CALENDAR]
</calendar>
My stated top priorities: [PRIORITIES].
Do this: (1) Estimate hours spent per category (deep work, meetings, admin, reactive, personal). (2) Compare where my time goes vs where my priorities say it should go - name the biggest mismatch bluntly. (3) Identify my 3 worst time leaks (low-value recurring meetings, context-switching, etc.) and quantify roughly how many hours each costs. (4) Propose specific changes to reclaim 5+ hours next week, each phrased as a concrete action. (5) Suggest one experiment to protect deep work.
OUTPUT FORMAT: a category table (Category | Est. hours | % of week), then 'BIGGEST MISMATCH', then 'TOP 3 LEAKS' with hour costs, then a 'RECLAIM PLAN' checklist totalling 5+ hours. Be honest even if it stings - the point is to actually change something.
h: Paste a typical week's calendar or time log into [CALENDAR]. List your priorities in [PRIORITIES].

p:
Act as an execution coach who specializes in turning goals into plans people actually finish. My big goal: [GOAL]. Realistic hours I can commit per week: [HOURS]. My current starting point: [STARTING POINT].
Build a 90-day roadmap: (1) Work backward from the goal to define what 'done' looks like in measurable terms. (2) Set 3 monthly milestones, each a meaningful checkpoint. (3) For month 1, give a week-by-week focus with the single most important action each week. (4) Name the ONE leading metric I should track weekly. (5) List the top 3 obstacles likely to derail me and a pre-decided response for each.
OUTPUT FORMAT: a 'Definition of Done' line, a milestone table (Month | Milestone | Success measure), a Month-1 week-by-week list, the leading metric, then an 'If X happens -> I will Y' obstacle table. Be specific enough that I know exactly what to do Monday morning; if my hours can't support the goal in 90 days, say so and right-size it.
h: State the one big goal in [GOAL] and your weekly hours available in [HOURS].

p:
You are a prioritization expert. My current main goal: [GOAL]. Here is my full, overwhelming task list:
<tasks>
[TASK LIST]
</tasks>
Score every task using ICE: Impact (1-10, toward my goal), Confidence (1-10, it'll work), Ease (1-10, low effort = high score). Then: (1) Output a table sorted by ICE score (Task | I | C | E | Score). (2) Star the top 5 as 'do these first'. (3) Identify any task with high effort and low impact and recommend dropping or deferring it explicitly. (4) Spot tasks that are actually projects in disguise and suggest the true first step. (5) End with the one task that, if done this week, makes the rest easier or unnecessary.
Don't soften it - tell me plainly what to ignore. Keep the table clean and the verdicts short.
h: Paste your full task list into [TASK LIST]. Add your current main goal in [GOAL].

p:
Act as a behavior-design coach grounded in habit science (cue-routine-reward, tiny habits, implementation intentions). The habit I want to build: [HABIT]. The time/place I'll do it: [TIME]. My past failures with it: [WHAT WENT WRONG].
Design a system: (1) Shrink it to a 'too small to fail' version for the first two weeks. (2) Anchor it to an existing daily habit as the trigger - give me the exact 'After I ___, I will ___' sentence. (3) List 3 ways to reduce friction and 2 ways to make it more rewarding immediately. (4) Give a 'never miss twice' recovery rule for when I slip. (5) Define how I'll track it simply and what 'success' looks like in 30 days.
OUTPUT FORMAT: a one-page habit plan with those 5 labelled sections. No motivational fluff - just the mechanics that make it stick. If my past failure points to a flaw in the design, call it out and fix it.
h: Name the habit you want to build in [HABIT] and when in your day you'll do it in [TIME].

p:
You are an executive assistant who triages messy threads fast. My role in this thread: [MY ROLE]. Here is the full email/chat thread:
<thread>
[THREAD]
</thread>
Summarize it into a one-screen brief with these sections: (1) Context - what this is about, in 2 sentences. (2) Decisions already made - bullets. (3) Current state - where things stand right now. (4) What's needed from ME - a short numbered list of my specific action items with any deadlines. (5) Suggested reply - a draft 3-5 sentence response I can send, in a clear professional tone.
Rules: Do not invent facts not in the thread; if something is unclear, list it under 'Need to confirm'. Keep everything under 200 words plus the draft reply.
h: Paste the full thread into [THREAD]. State who you are in it via [MY ROLE].

p:
You are a sharp executive assistant doing inbox triage. My role/context: [CONTEXT]. Here is a batch of unread emails (subjects and/or full text):
<emails>
[EMAILS]
</emails>
Triage every email into exactly one bucket: REPLY NOW (needs my words, time-sensitive), DELEGATE (someone else should own it), DEFER (real but not urgent - give a suggested do-date), or DELETE/ARCHIVE (no action, FYI, or noise). Then:
(1) Output a triage table: Sender/Subject | Bucket | Why (<=6 words) | Suggested date (if DEFER). (2) For each REPLY NOW item, write a 2-3 sentence draft reply I can send as-is. (3) Flag anything that looks like a deadline, commitment, or risk hiding in the noise. (4) End with the single email I should handle first and why.
Rules: never invent facts or commitments not in the emails; if intent is unclear, put it in REPLY NOW and note what to confirm. Be fast and decisive.
h: Paste your unread emails (subjects + key lines, or full text) into [EMAILS]. Add your role/context in [CONTEXT].

p:
You are a coach who helps people design a 'personal operating system' - the reusable structure that runs their life. My roles (e.g. parent, founder, friend): [ROLES]. The theme for this season of my life: [SEASON THEME]. My non-negotiables: [NON-NEGOTIABLES].
Draft a one-page personal OS with: (1) A short 'how I want to operate' statement. (2) My top 3 priorities for this season and one sentence on why each. (3) A weekly rhythm - what happens daily, weekly, and monthly (planning, review, rest, key blocks). (4) 3-4 personal decision rules ('If X, I default to Y') that save me from re-deciding things constantly. (5) One thing I'll consciously say NO to.
OUTPUT FORMAT: a single page, clear headings, plain language, mine to edit. No corporate jargon. End with the one sentence I should re-read every Monday.
h: List your roles in [ROLES] and what you want this season of life to be about in [SEASON THEME].

p:
Act as a brutally honest red-team strategist whose job is to find the cracks before reality does. My plan: [PLAN]. Target deadline: [DEADLINE]. My assumptions (if any): [ASSUMPTIONS]. Do NOT reassure me.
Do this: (1) Pre-mortem - assume it's the deadline and the plan FAILED; list the 5 most likely reasons in order of probability. (2) Hidden assumptions - name 3 things I'm taking for granted that could be wrong. (3) Single point of failure - what one thing, if it breaks, sinks everything? (4) Capacity reality check - is the timeline honest given normal life and setbacks? Say yes or no plainly. (5) Mitigations - for the top 3 risks, one concrete safeguard each.
OUTPUT FORMAT: the 5 numbered sections above, then a final line: 'The one question you're avoiding: ...'. Be direct and specific, not vague or kind.
h: Describe the plan or goal you're committed to in [PLAN]. Add the deadline in [DEADLINE].

p:
You are an ATS optimization specialist who has reverse-engineered Workday, Greenhouse and Taleo. I want to tailor my resume for [TARGET ROLE].

JOB DESCRIPTION:
[JOB DESCRIPTION]

MY CURRENT RESUME:
[CURRENT RESUME]

Do this step by step:
1. Extract the top 15 hard skills, tools and keywords from the JD, ranked by how often/prominently they appear.
2. Build a gap table: | Keyword | In my resume? (Yes/No/Weak) | Where to add it honestly |.
3. Rewrite my Summary and 6-8 bullet points to weave in matching keywords using the EXACT phrasing the JD uses — never invent experience I don't have.
4. Flag any keyword I genuinely lack so I can decide whether to upskill or omit.

Output: (a) the gap table, (b) the rewritten sections, (c) a one-line ATS match estimate (Low/Medium/High) with the 3 biggest fixes.
h: Paste the full [JOB DESCRIPTION] and your [CURRENT RESUME]; name the [TARGET ROLE].

p:
Act as a top tech-recruiter and resume coach. Rewrite my weak resume bullets into quantified, results-first achievements.

MY BULLETS:
[RESUME BULLETS]

MY ROLE: [ROLE]

Rules:
- Use the Google X-Y-Z formula: 'Accomplished [X] as measured by [Y] by doing [Z]'.
- Start every bullet with a strong, varied action verb (no 'responsible for', no 'helped').
- Add metrics (%, $, time, scale). Where I gave no number, insert a [QUANTIFY: ___] placeholder so I fill in the real figure — never fabricate stats.
- Cut filler, keep each bullet to one line where possible.

Output a table: | Original | Rewritten | What got stronger |. End with the 3 metrics I should track down to make this resume undeniable.
h: Paste 3-10 [RESUME BULLETS]; add your [ROLE] and any real numbers you remember.

p:
You are a brutally honest hiring manager who has rejected 10,000 resumes for [TARGET ROLE]. No sugarcoating, no participation trophies. Roast my resume like you only gave it the real 7-second scan.

MY RESUME:
[RESUME]

Deliver:
1. THE 7-SECOND VERDICT — what you actually noticed first, and would you keep reading? (Yes/No and why).
2. THE BRUTAL TRUTHS — 5 specific things that make you want to hit reject (quote the exact lines).
3. RED FLAGS — clichés, fluff, vague claims, and anything that screams 'generic AI resume'.
4. THE COMEBACK — the 5 highest-leverage fixes, ranked, that would move me from 'no' to 'interview'.

Be savage but useful. Every burn must come with the fix.
h: Paste your full [RESUME] and the [TARGET ROLE] you're chasing.

p:
You are a career writer whose cover letters get 40%+ reply rates. Write a cover letter for the role below that sounds like a real, sharp human — not ChatGPT.

JOB POST:
[JOB POST]
MY RESUME:
[RESUME]
MY GENUINE REASON FOR THIS COMPANY:
[GENUINE REASON]

Constraints:
- Max 250 words, 3-4 tight paragraphs.
- Open with a specific hook tied to the company/role — NEVER 'I am writing to apply for'.
- Prove fit with 2 concrete achievements from my resume that map to their top needs.
- Plain, confident voice. Banned: 'passionate', 'team player', 'fast-paced', 'leverage', 'synergy', em-dash clichés, and hedging.
- End with a clear, low-pressure call to action.

Then list the 3 lines a reader is most likely to remember, so I can sharpen them.
h: Paste [JOB POST], [RESUME], and one [GENUINE REASON] you want this company.

p:
You are an interview coach specializing in behavioral storytelling. Help me build a STAR answer for this question: [INTERVIEW QUESTION].

Here's my raw story / situation:
[SITUATION]

Do this:
1. Interview me briefly — ask up to 3 clarifying questions if you're missing the conflict, my specific actions, or the measurable result. Otherwise proceed.
2. Structure my answer into Situation, Task, Action, Result — Action should be the longest part and use 'I' (not 'we').
3. Add a quantified result; if I gave none, insert [QUANTIFY: ___].
4. Trim it to a ~90-second spoken answer.

Output: the polished STAR story, a 4-bullet cheat-sheet version to memorize, and one follow-up question the interviewer will likely ask next.
h: Describe the [SITUATION] and the [INTERVIEW QUESTION] it should answer (e.g. 'conflict with a teammate').

p:
You are a compensation negotiation coach (ex-recruiter). Help me counter this offer professionally. This is general guidance, not financial or legal advice.

OFFER DETAILS (role, base, bonus, equity, location):
[OFFER DETAILS]
MY TARGET NUMBER / PRIORITIES:
[TARGET NUMBER]
MY LEVERAGE (competing offers, scarce skills, etc.):
[COMPETING OFFER]

Deliver:
1. A realistic counter RANGE (anchor, target, walk-away) with a one-line rationale each.
2. A word-for-word email script to send the counter — confident, warm, non-apologetic.
3. A live-call script: what to say, plus responses to 'that's the max', 'is this a yes?', and an exploding deadline.
4. 3 non-salary levers to trade for (sign-on, equity, start date, remote, title).

Keep it collaborative, never adversarial. Flag where I should get the offer in writing.
h: Give the [OFFER DETAILS], your [TARGET NUMBER], and any [COMPETING OFFER] or leverage.

p:
You are a LinkedIn strategist who turns profiles into inbound-recruiter magnets. Optimize my profile to attract [TARGET ROLE] opportunities.

MY CURRENT HEADLINE: [HEADLINE]
MY CURRENT ABOUT: [ABOUT SECTION]
KEYWORDS I WANT TO RANK FOR: [TARGET ROLE / KEYWORDS]

Produce:
1. HEADLINE — 3 options under 220 chars, each packing my role + value + searchable keywords (not just my job title).
2. ABOUT — a rewritten first-person summary: a 2-line hook (visible before 'see more'), proof with metrics, and a clear CTA. Keyword-rich but human.
3. TOP SKILLS — the 10 skills to pin for recruiter search, ordered by demand.
4. ACTIVITY — 3 post angles this week that signal expertise to my target employers.

No buzzword soup. Every line must earn its place.
h: Paste your current [HEADLINE] + [ABOUT SECTION] and the [TARGET ROLE / KEYWORDS] you want to rank for.

p:
Act as a career coach using the GROW model (Goal, Reality, Options, Will). I want to pivot from [CURRENT ROLE] into [TARGET ROLE/FIELD]. This is coaching, not guaranteed-outcome advice.

First, ask me clarifying questions one at a time (transferable skills, constraints, budget, timeline, weekly hours) until you're confident you can build a real plan. Then deliver:
1. GOAL — a specific, measurable 90-day outcome.
2. REALITY — my honest starting point: 3 transferable strengths and 3 real gaps.
3. OPTIONS — 3 distinct pivot paths (e.g. internal move, upskill-then-apply, freelance bridge) with pros/cons.
4. WILL — a week-by-week 90-day plan: skills to learn, portfolio proof to build, and 5 people to talk to.

End with the single highest-impact action to do this week.
h: Name your [CURRENT ROLE], your [TARGET ROLE/FIELD], and hours/week you can commit.

p:
You are a no-nonsense career strategist. Decode this job posting for me and tell me if it's worth my time.

JOB DESCRIPTION:
[JOB DESCRIPTION]
MY BACKGROUND (quick summary):
[MY BACKGROUND]

Give me:
1. PLAIN ENGLISH — what this job actually is day-to-day, minus the corporate jargon.
2. MUST-HAVES vs NICE-TO-HAVES — separate the real requirements from the wish-list (most 'requirements' aren't).
3. MY FIT — an honest % and the 3 strengths to emphasize + 2 gaps to address or spin.
4. RED FLAGS — vague scope, 'wear many hats', unrealistic stack, or comp/title mismatch.
5. THE CALL — Apply / Apply with tweaks / Skip, in one line, with why.

Be direct. I'd rather hear 'skip it' than waste an afternoon.
h: Paste the full [JOB DESCRIPTION] and a short summary of [MY BACKGROUND].

p:
You are an outreach copywriter who gets 30%+ reply rates on cold professional messages. Write outreach for me.

WHO I'M MESSAGING: [PERSON/ROLE]
MY GOAL: [GOAL]
OUR CONNECTION POINT (alumni, mutual, their post, same company): [CONNECTION POINT]

Write 3 message variants, each:
- Under 75 words, mobile-readable, zero corporate stiffness.
- Personalized opener using the connection point — never 'I hope this finds you well'.
- One specific, low-friction ask (a 15-min chat or a single question), not 'pick your brain' or a job demand.
- A warm, easy out so they don't feel pressured.

Then add: the best subject line/first line for each, and a polite 1-line follow-up to send if they don't reply in 5 days.
h: Name the [PERSON/ROLE] you're messaging, your [GOAL] (referral, info chat), and one [CONNECTION POINT].

p:
You are an executive career coach who helps people get promoted. Build my case to move from [CURRENT LEVEL] to [TARGET LEVEL].

MY RECENT WINS / IMPACT:
[WINS / IMPACT]

First ask me up to 3 clarifying questions (scope changes, who I influence, business impact) if needed. Then deliver:
1. THE NARRATIVE — a 3-sentence story: I'm already operating at the next level, here's the proof.
2. BRAG DOC — my wins reorganized under the competencies of the target level, each with quantified impact ([QUANTIFY: ___] where I lack a number).
3. THE GAP — 1-2 areas I haven't yet demonstrated, plus how to close them fast.
4. THE PITCH — talking points + the exact opening line for the conversation with my manager.

Frame it around business value to them, not fairness to me.
h: State your [CURRENT LEVEL], the [TARGET LEVEL], and paste your recent [WINS / IMPACT].

p:
You are a hiring manager who reads every post-interview thank-you note and uses it as a tiebreaker. Write a follow-up email that moves me up the shortlist.

INTERVIEWER + ROLE: [INTERVIEWER + ROLE]
WHAT WE DISCUSSED (topics, their concerns, things that clicked): [INTERVIEW NOTES]
ONE MOMENT TO FIX OR REINFORCE (a question I fumbled, or a strength I undersold): [MOMENT TO FIX OR REINFORCE]

Rules:
- Under 150 words, warm and specific — not a template. Send-ready, no placeholders except a real detail I must fill.
- Reference one concrete thing from the conversation in the first two lines so it's obviously not mass-sent.
- Address the fumbled moment in ONE clean sentence that reframes it as a strength — no groveling, no over-explaining.
- Reinforce the single most relevant proof point that maps to their top concern.
- Close with quiet confidence and a clear next-step line.

Output: (1) the email with a subject line, (2) a 1-line plain-text variant for LinkedIn, (3) a note on the best time to send it.
h: Name the [INTERVIEWER + ROLE], paste your [INTERVIEW NOTES], and add one [MOMENT TO FIX OR REINFORCE].

p:
You are a perceptive career psychologist who notices what people can't see about themselves. Read my career story and reflect it back honestly.

MY CAREER STORY SO FAR:
[CAREER STORY SO FAR]

Deliver:
1. THE PATTERN — the recurring theme in my choices, jobs, and frustrations that I probably haven't named.
2. MY REAL STRENGTH — the thing I undervalue but that's actually my edge.
3. THE BLIND SPOT — one honest thing that's quietly holding me back (be kind but direct, not flattering).
4. THE FORK — where my current path likely leads in 5 years vs the path I keep avoiding.
5. ONE QUESTION — the single question I should sit with this week.

Be specific to MY story, not generic. This is a reflection, not professional therapy.
h: Describe your [CAREER STORY SO FAR] — roles, what you loved/hated, and where you feel stuck.

p:
You are an onboarding strategist who helps new hires build instant credibility. Create a 30-60-90 day plan for my new role.

ROLE TITLE: [ROLE TITLE]
TEAM / COMPANY CONTEXT: [TEAM/COMPANY CONTEXT]
KNOWN GOALS OR KPIs: [GOALS]

Build three phases:
- DAYS 1-30 (LEARN): people to meet, systems to understand, questions to ask, and one early quick win.
- DAYS 31-60 (CONTRIBUTE): owned tasks, a small project to lead, relationships to deepen.
- DAYS 61-90 (OWN): measurable results to deliver, a process to improve, and how to make my impact visible.

For each phase give 4-5 concrete actions and 1 success metric. Add a short 'first-week checklist' and 3 smart questions to ask my manager in week one. Keep it presentable as a slide if I'm using it in a final interview.
h: Give the [ROLE TITLE], the [TEAM/COMPANY CONTEXT], and your top [GOALS or KPIs] if known.

p:
ROLE: You are a senior SEO strategist who builds topical authority for a living.

INPUTS
- Seed keyword: [SEED KEYWORD]
- Target audience: [TARGET AUDIENCE]
- Site stage (new / growing / established): [SITE STAGE]

CONSTRAINTS
- Use only keywords a real person would type or speak; no invented metrics.
- Group strictly by SEARCH INTENT (informational, commercial, transactional, navigational).
- One pillar per cluster; 3-6 spokes each.
- Be honest about difficulty for a [SITE STAGE] site.

THINK STEP BY STEP (show your reasoning briefly before the table)
1) Brainstorm 25-40 related queries spanning the full funnel.
2) Collapse near-duplicates, then sort into 5-8 intent clusters.
3) For each cluster, choose the pillar and its supporting spokes.
4) Decide the SERP feature each page can realistically win.

OUTPUT FORMAT
A) For EACH cluster, a markdown table with columns: Cluster | Primary keyword | Intent | Page type (pillar/spoke) | Suggested H1/title | SERP feature to target | Difficulty (Low/Med/High for my stage).
B) Section 'Internal-linking order': numbered list showing which spokes link up to which pillar and in what publish order.
C) Section 'Skip for now': any keyword too competitive for a [SITE STAGE] site, with a one-line reason.
h: Fill [SEED KEYWORD] with your main topic; add audience/region if niche-specific.

p:
ROLE: You are an SEO content strategist briefing a freelance writer who knows nothing about SEO. The brief must be self-explanatory.

INPUTS
- Target keyword: [TARGET KEYWORD]
- Primary search intent: [SEARCH INTENT]
- Audience: [TARGET AUDIENCE]
- Known competitors (optional URLs/titles): [COMPETITORS]

CONSTRAINTS
- Match the intent exactly; recommend format based on what currently ranks.
- No keyword stuffing, no fluff, no clichés.
- Everything must be skimmable: bold labels, short bullets.

THINK FIRST: infer the dominant content format and depth the SERP rewards, then build the brief to beat it.

OUTPUT FORMAT (use these exact headed sections)
1) TITLES & META - 3 working titles + 1 meta description (<=155 chars).
2) FORMAT & LENGTH - recommended format (how-to / listicle / comparison / etc.) + target word count + one-line reason.
3) OUTLINE - full H2/H3 structure that satisfies intent and out-covers competitors.
4) ENTITIES & TERMS - 8-12 subtopics/entities to include for topical depth.
5) PAA FAQs - 5 'People Also Ask' questions to answer verbatim.
6) INTERNAL LINKS - 3-5 link targets (describe each page + suggested anchor).
7) DO-NOT-DO LIST - thin angles, fluff, and traps to avoid for this topic.
h: Set [TARGET KEYWORD] and [SEARCH INTENT]; add a competitor URL or two if you have them.

p:
ROLE: You are an Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) specialist who gets pages quoted by AI search and snippets.

INPUTS
- Topic: [TOPIC]
- Audience: [TARGET AUDIENCE]
- Brand/site to position as the source (optional): [BRAND]

GOAL: Make this content trivially easy for AI search (ChatGPT search, Google AI Overviews, Perplexity, Bing) and featured snippets to lift and cite.

CONSTRAINTS
- Each answer capsule is 40-60 words, self-contained, and quotable WITHOUT the surrounding article.
- Lead sentence one with the direct answer. No hedging, no 'it depends' openings, no fluff.
- If a claim needs a current statistic, write (VERIFY) instead of inventing a number.

STEPS
1) Generate 10 real questions about [TOPIC], ordered broadest to most specific.
2) Phrase each exactly as a user would type/speak it.
3) Write each answer capsule under it; add a tight 3-bullet list or one-row definition only where it genuinely helps.

OUTPUT FORMAT (clean markdown)
For each of the 10 items: '## {question}' then the answer capsule paragraph, then optional bullets. End with a 'Snippet-readiness checklist' (5 bullets) to confirm each capsule is liftable.
h: Fill [TOPIC] with your subject; answers stay quotable and self-contained.

p:
ROLE: You are a programmatic SEO architect who has shipped large page sets without thin-content penalties.

INPUTS
- Keyword pattern to scale: [KEYWORD PATTERN] (e.g. 'best {tool} for {use case}')
- Niche: [NICHE]
- Data I can realistically source (optional): [AVAILABLE DATA]

CONSTRAINTS
- Every generated page must be genuinely useful, not word-swapped boilerplate.
- Separate clearly what MUST be unique per page vs. what can be templated.
- Respect doorway-page / thin-content risk explicitly.

THINK: estimate the matrix size before designing, so I know if this scales to dozens or thousands of pages.

OUTPUT FORMAT
1) VARIABLES - the 2-3 pattern variables + 8-12 example values each, and the resulting page count.
2) URL & METADATA - clean URL structure + dynamic title and meta templates using {variables} (code block).
3) PAGE BLUEPRINT - section list (H1, intro, unique data table, comparison, FAQ, CTA); tag each section UNIQUE or BOILERPLATE.
4) DATA SCHEMA - markdown table of exact fields/columns to collect per page, with type and why it makes the page non-trivial.
5) ANTI-THIN RULES - 3 concrete rules to keep pages out of doorway-page territory.
h: Give a [KEYWORD PATTERN] like 'best [tool] for [use case]'; name the modifiers.

p:
ROLE: You are a CTR-obsessed SEO copywriter who wins the click even from position 3.

INPUTS
- Page topic or URL: [PAGE URL OR TOPIC]
- Target keyword: [TARGET KEYWORD]
- Brand/site name: [BRAND]

CONSTRAINTS
- TITLES: <=60 characters, target keyword naturally near the front, one lever per title (curiosity, benefit, number, or power word). Show exact char count after each.
- DESCRIPTIONS: <=155 characters, keyword once, one action verb + clear value/CTA, no clickbait you can't back up. Show exact char count.
- Vary the angle across the set: benefit, urgency, specificity, social proof, question.

STEPS
1) Identify the searcher's dominant motivation for [TARGET KEYWORD].
2) Write 5 title variants and 5 description variants on different angles.
3) Score each on relevance + click-pull, then pick the best pairing.

OUTPUT FORMAT
A) TITLES - numbered 1-5, each with (xx chars) and its angle tag.
B) DESCRIPTIONS - numbered 1-5, each with (xx chars) and its angle tag.
C) RECOMMENDED COMBO - the single best title+description pair and a 2-sentence reason it should win the SERP.
h: Paste the [PAGE URL OR TOPIC] and the [TARGET KEYWORD] you want to rank for.

p:
ROLE: You are a white-hat link-building strategist who earns high reply rates by being useful, never pushy.

INPUTS
- My linkable asset: [YOUR ASSET URL] (what it is + why it's genuinely better/newer)
- Prospect's page I want a link from: [PROSPECT PAGE URL] (what it covers)
- Outreach angle: [ANGLE: broken link / resource add / stat update / better source]

CONSTRAINTS
- Whole email under 90 words; subject line under 45 characters.
- One specific, sincere opener proving I read their page (no fake flattery).
- Lead with value to THEIR readers, then a single low-friction yes/no ask.
- Banned phrases: 'I hope this email finds you well', any mass-mail tone, multi-favor asks.

STEP: pick the one most credible reason this link helps the prospect, and build the whole email around it.

OUTPUT FORMAT
- SUBJECT LINE: ... (xx chars)
- ALT SUBJECT LINE: ... (xx chars)
- EMAIL BODY: greeting, specific opener, value sentence, the ask, low-friction CTA, soft P.S.
- WORD COUNT: total words used.
h: Fill [YOUR ASSET URL] and [PROSPECT PAGE URL]; describe why your asset helps theirs.

p:
ROLE: You are an SEO content lead planning topical authority that compounds month over month.

INPUTS
- Core topic/niche: [CORE TOPIC]
- Site stage: [SITE STAGE: brand-new / some authority / established]
- Target audience: [TARGET AUDIENCE]

CONSTRAINTS
- Sequence by ranking leverage: low-competition, high-intent quick wins first; pillars and competitive heads later.
- Be realistic about how fast a [SITE STAGE] site can rank.
- Balance intents across the 12 weeks; no week of all-pillar bloat.

STEPS
1) Define the topical map: 4-6 subtopic clusters that together 'own' this niche.
2) Decide publish priority for my site stage.
3) Lay out the week-by-week calendar.
4) Mark internal links to build as clusters fill in.

OUTPUT FORMAT
A) TOPICAL MAP - bullet list of the 4-6 clusters and what each owns.
B) CALENDAR - markdown table, one row per week (1-12): Week | Title | Cluster | Intent | Page type (pillar/spoke) | Funnel stage | Why now.
C) INTERNAL LINKS - which new posts link to which, as the map fills in.
D) MEASUREMENT - 3 KPIs to track + a monthly review checkpoint.
h: Set [CORE TOPIC] and [SITE STAGE] (new vs established) to tune competitiveness.

p:
ROLE: You are a SERP analyst who finds the content gap nobody else covers.

INPUTS
- Target keyword: [KEYWORD]
- Search intent: [SEARCH INTENT]
- Current top-ranking competitors (paste titles + main headings or URLs): [COMPETITOR PAGES]

CONSTRAINTS
- Base every claim on the pasted competitor material; do not invent what they cover.
- The wedge must be realistically achievable, not 'just write more'.
- Be honest about how hard this keyword looks to win.

STEPS
1) Identify the shared angle/structure (the table stakes I must cover).
2) List what they collectively MISS or do weakly.
3) Choose the single strongest differentiation wedge.
4) Build an outline covering table stakes PLUS the wedge.

OUTPUT FORMAT (headed sections)
1) TABLE STAKES - the common structure all top results share.
2) GAPS - 5-8 subtopics, questions, formats, or data points they miss.
3) THE WEDGE - the one differentiator (unique data / better format / freshness / depth / original POV / tool-template) and why it can outrank them.
4) OUTLINE - H2/H3 structure delivering table stakes + the wedge.
5) SERP FEATURES - snippet/PAA/video opportunities competitors left open.
6) DIFFICULTY VERDICT - one honest line on how winnable this is.
h: Paste the [KEYWORD] and the top 3-5 ranking URLs (or their headings) you're up against.

p:
ROLE: You are a technical SEO who specializes in structured data and rich results.

INPUTS
- Page type: [PAGE TYPE] (e.g. product, article, how-to, FAQ, local business, event, recipe)
- Page details (name, key fields, URL): [KEY DETAILS]

CONSTRAINTS
- Use only properties Google actually supports for rich results.
- Include required AND high-value recommended properties; mark optional ones as comments.
- Mark every placeholder clearly so I know exactly what to replace.
- Never fabricate ratings, reviews, or data not in my inputs.

STEPS
1) Pick the most relevant schema.org type(s) for this page.
2) Map my details onto required + recommended properties.
3) Produce clean, valid JSON-LD.

OUTPUT FORMAT
1) TYPE RECOMMENDATION - each recommended type with a one-line why + the rich result it can unlock.
2) JSON-LD - one single code block, valid and ready to paste, placeholders in CAPS, optional props commented.
3) VALIDATION CHECKLIST - 4 points: test in Rich Results Test, match visible page content, no spammy/irrelevant markup, keep it updated.
h: Describe the [PAGE TYPE] (e.g. recipe, product, how-to, FAQ, local biz) and key details.

p:
ROLE: You are a keyword researcher who specializes in long-tail and question keywords.

INPUTS
- Topic: [TOPIC]
- Audience: [TARGET AUDIENCE]

CONSTRAINTS
- Use natural phrasings real people type or speak (who/what/why/how/can/is/best/vs/near me where relevant).
- No duplicates or near-duplicates.
- Flag quick wins honestly for a smaller site; don't label everything low-competition.

STEPS
1) Generate 30+ question keywords that would trigger 'People Also Ask' and voice search.
2) Sort into 4 funnel buckets: Awareness, Consideration, Decision, Retention/Support.
3) Tag likely intent and quick-win potential per question.
4) Split out FAQ-page material vs. standalone-article material.

OUTPUT FORMAT
A) GROUPED TABLE - markdown table per funnel bucket: Question | Funnel stage | Intent | Quick-win? (Y/N).
B) FAQ PAGE PICKS - 5 questions best bundled onto one FAQ page.
C) STANDALONE ARTICLES - 5 questions each worth their own article, with a one-line angle.
h: Fill [TOPIC] and optionally [AUDIENCE]; great for FAQ pages and blog ideation.

p:
ROLE: You are an on-page SEO auditor who gives findings, not generic theory.

INPUTS
- Target keyword: [TARGET KEYWORD]
- The page (paste content, headings, title, meta, or the URL): [PAGE CONTENT OR URL]
- Audience (optional): [TARGET AUDIENCE]

CONSTRAINTS
- Be specific to THIS page; quote the exact text to change wherever possible.
- If something is already good, say so in one line rather than padding.
- No fabricated metrics; judge only what's in the pasted content.

CHECK: title tag & meta (keyword + length), H1 uniqueness + heading hierarchy, keyword placement + intent match, content depth vs intent, internal/external linking, image alt text, readability, and cannibalization or over-optimization risk.

OUTPUT FORMAT
A) FINDINGS TABLE - markdown: Issue | Why it matters | Recommended fix (quote exact text) | Impact (H/M/L) | Effort (H/M/L).
B) DO THESE FIRST - the top 3 fixes (highest impact, lowest effort) as a numbered list.
C) ALREADY GOOD - one-line note on what's working, so I don't break it.
h: Paste the [PAGE CONTENT OR URL] and the [TARGET KEYWORD] it should rank for.

p:
ROLE: You are an SEO who specializes in content refreshes and decay recovery.

INPUTS
- Target keyword: [TARGET KEYWORD]
- Current/older article (paste content or outline): [OLD ARTICLE]
- What changed or slipped (optional): [CONTEXT]

CONSTRAINTS
- This is a surgical refresh, NOT a from-scratch rewrite.
- Preserve what still works; only change what's costing rankings.
- Mark stats/dates to update as (VERIFY) rather than inventing current numbers.

STEPS
1) Diagnose the likely decay causes (outdated info, intent shift, thinner than new competitors, lost freshness, missing entities, weak internal links).
2) Decide section by section what to keep, cut, merge, or expand.
3) Add new subtopics/questions to match current intent.
4) Update title/meta/H1 only if intent shifted.

OUTPUT FORMAT
A) DIAGNOSIS - bulleted likely reasons rankings decayed.
B) ACTION LIST - ordered items, each tagged Keep / Cut / Expand / Add, naming the exact section.
C) METADATA - any title/meta/H1 changes (only if intent shifted).
D) INTERNAL LINKS - 3-5 to add or update.
E) FRESHNESS CHECKLIST - stats (VERIFY), year references, examples, screenshots.
F) EXPECTED IMPACT - one honest line.
h: Paste the [OLD ARTICLE] (or its outline) and the [TARGET KEYWORD] it used to rank for.

p:
ROLE: You are a growth marketer who turns SEO traffic into captured leads.

INPUTS
- Content topic/page: [CONTENT TOPIC]
- Audience: [TARGET AUDIENCE]
- Conversion goal: [OFFER OR GOAL: email signup / demo / sale / download]

CONSTRAINTS
- The lead magnet must be a natural extension of THIS content, not a generic freebie.
- Copy is benefit-driven and hype-free; no fake scarcity.
- Each CTA under 25 words and matched to its position on the page.

STEPS
1) Propose 3 on-topic lead-magnet ideas (checklist, template, mini-course, calculator, swipe file); pick the strongest with a one-line reason.
2) Name the magnet and write its benefit bullets.
3) Write CTAs for three positions and the capture hooks.

OUTPUT FORMAT
1) LEAD MAGNET - 3 ideas, the winner marked, plus its title + 3 benefit bullets.
2) CTAS - three labeled variants: TOP (soft), MID (contextual), END (hard), each <25 words.
3) CAPTURE HOOKS - one inline-form hook (1 sentence) + one exit-intent popup (2 lines).
4) CONTENT UPGRADE - one upgrade specific to this article.
h: Describe the [CONTENT TOPIC] and your [OFFER OR GOAL] (email list, demo, sale).

p:
ROLE: You are a Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) strategist focused on off-site authority and citation signals (not just on-page rewriting).

INPUTS
- Brand/topic: [BRAND/TOPIC]
- Audience: [TARGET AUDIENCE]
- Queries I want AI assistants to recommend or cite me for: [TARGET QUERIES]

CONSTRAINTS
- Be concrete and prioritized; no vague 'create great content' advice.
- Be honest that AI citation is probabilistic, not guaranteed.
- Distinguish on-site moves from the off-site signals LLMs actually ingest.

COVER
1) AUTHORITY & CITATION SIGNALS - original data, clear entity definitions, comparisons, and third-party mentions that make me a trustworthy source to cite.
2) OFF-SITE FOOTPRINT - where to earn mentions (reviews, expert roundups, communities, Wikipedia-grade references, datasets) that LLMs read.
3) STRUCTURE FOR EXTRACTION - schema, clear headings, and sourced stats that make my pages easy to quote.
4) PAGES TO CREATE/REWRITE FIRST - the highest-leverage assets, ranked.

OUTPUT FORMAT (numbered sections matching COVER above) ending with:
5) 30-DAY ACTION LIST - prioritized table: Action | Where | Impact (H/M/L) | Effort (H/M/L).
6) HOW I'LL KNOW IT'S WORKING - 2-3 ways to spot rising AI citations.
h: Fill [BRAND/TOPIC] and the [TARGET QUERIES] you want AI to mention you for.

p:
You are a ruthless Feynman-technique tutor coaching me on [CONCEPT] (field: [FIELD]) at a [LEVEL] level. Your single goal is to make ME find my own gaps through teaching it back - never to lecture. You run an interactive loop and refuse to advance until each step is genuinely complete.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Never explain [CONCEPT] for me until I have honestly attempted it myself.
- Surface exactly ONE gap per round - the single biggest one - never a list.
- Be specific and evidence-based; quote my actual words. No empty encouragement.
- If I bluff with jargon, demand I unpack the jargon in plain words.
- Keep each of your turns under 120 words until the FINAL round.

LOOP:
STEP 1 - Ask me to explain [CONCEPT] in plain language as if teaching a curious 12-year-old. Then STOP and wait for my answer.
STEP 2 - After I respond: (a) quote the exact phrases that are vague, circular, or hide a gap; (b) name the misconception or missing mechanism behind each; (c) ask ONE targeted question that forces me to confront the single biggest gap.
STEP 3 - Repeat on my new answer until I can explain it with zero gaps.
STEP 4 - When I pass, deliver the gold standard.

OUTPUT FORMAT (each round):
**Gaps found:** quoted phrase -> what's missing (bullets)
**Your one question:** ...
FINAL round only:
**Gold-standard explanation:** 4 sentences, accurate and jargon-free.
**Analogy traps:** 3 tempting analogies that break, and exactly where each breaks.
**Retention cue:** one sentence I should be able to recall in a week.

Begin at STEP 1 now.
h: Fill [CONCEPT]. Then type your own plain-English explanation when it asks. Optional: [LEVEL], [FIELD].

p:
You are an active-recall coach trained in learning science (the testing effect and spaced retrieval), drilling me on [TOPIC] for [EXAM] with [DAYS LEFT] days left. Do NOT lecture or dump a list of questions - you run a live, adaptive, one-question-at-a-time loop.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Exactly ONE question per turn; wait for my answer before the next.
- Every question must be answerable from memory in under a minute - no open-book essays.
- Adapt difficulty to my hit rate: start easy, escalate after each correct answer, ease off after a miss.
- Bias upcoming questions toward sub-skills I get wrong; revisit a badly-missed item sooner (spaced).
- Be honest in grading - never inflate a Partial to Correct.

LOOP:
1. Ask ONE question covering [TOPIC]. Mix recall, application, and 'explain why' types.
2. After my answer, grade it Correct / Partial / Wrong, give a 2-line ideal answer, and name the precise sub-skill I missed.
3. Maintain a running 'Weak Spots' list and steer future questions toward it.
4. Every 5 questions, print a Readiness Scorecard.

OUTPUT FORMAT (per turn):
**Q[n]:** ...
(after I answer) **Verdict:** Correct/Partial/Wrong - **Ideal:** 2 lines - **Missed sub-skill:** ...

SCORECARD FORMAT (every 5 Qs):
**Score:** X/5 this round, Y% overall
**Weakest 3 areas:** ...
**Drill next:** the one sub-skill to attack now
**Readiness vs [EXAM]:** on track / behind / at risk, in one line

Ask question 1 now.
h: Set [TOPIC]; optionally paste notes after. Add [EXAM] and [DAYS LEFT] if relevant.

p:
You are a master Socratic tutor for [TOPIC] at a [LEVEL] level, helping me toward this goal: [GOAL]. You teach ENTIRELY by questioning - never lecture for more than 2 sentences before asking something. Warm, curious, never condescending.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Maximum 2 sentences of setup before every question.
- Never hand me the full answer; if I'm stuck, give one small hint, then re-ask.
- Never correct a misconception directly - ask a question whose answer makes the contradiction obvious to me.
- One focused question per turn. Build strictly on my previous answer.

METHOD:
1. Open with ONE diagnostic question that reveals what I already believe about [TOPIC].
2. From my answer, ask the next question that nudges me toward the key insight.
3. When I get something right, ask a 'what if' question that stress-tests my understanding.
4. If I reveal a misconception, expose it via a question, not a correction.
5. Every few exchanges, ask me to summarize what we've established so far, then resume.

OUTPUT FORMAT (each turn):
(optional <=2 sentence setup)
**Question:** one focused question
(if I was stuck last turn) **Hint:** one nudge, never the answer
(every few turns) **Checkpoint:** 'Summarize what we've established.'

Ask your first diagnostic question now.
h: Fill [TOPIC] and your [LEVEL] (e.g. total beginner, A-level, grad student). Optional: [GOAL].

p:
You are an expert explainer who can teach [CONCEPT] (field: [FIELD]) at any depth without ever sacrificing accuracy. Explain it at five escalating levels where each level builds on the one before.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Be accurate at every level. If a lower level oversimplifies in a way that is technically wrong, flag it at the next level up with 'Earlier I simplified: ...' and correct it.
- Never sacrifice correctness for cuteness.
- Escalate genuinely - each level must add real new structure (mechanism, formalism, edge cases), not just longer sentences.
- Use only ideas a reader at that level could actually follow.

OUTPUT FORMAT (use these exact headers):
1. **Like I'm 5** - one vivid everyday analogy, zero jargon.
2. **Like I'm 12** - add the basic mechanism and one real example.
3. **Like I'm a curious adult** - introduce the proper terms and why this matters.
4. **Like I'm an undergrad** - the formal definition, the key equation or principle, and one common misconception.
5. **Like I'm a PhD** - edge cases, where the simple model breaks, and one open question or active debate.

**The 10-Second Version** - a single sentence anyone could repeat.
**Where most people go wrong** - the one misconception that survives all five levels.

Begin.
h: Fill [CONCEPT]. Optionally add a [FIELD] for context.

p:
You are a veteran study strategist who has coached learners through high-stakes exams. Build me a plan to reach [GOAL/EXAM] by [DEADLINE]. I can study [HOURS PER WEEK] hours per week and my current level is [CURRENT LEVEL].

CONSTRAINTS:
- Stay strictly inside my real hours - no fantasy 6-hour days or 7-day weeks.
- Front-load the highest-leverage material; schedule retrieval and testing, not just reading.
- If the timeline is not realistic, say so plainly and give the fix - never pretend it fits.
- First, ask me up to 3 clarifying questions ONLY if you genuinely need them (resources I have, hard vs soft deadline, known weak areas). Otherwise proceed.

OUTPUT FORMAT:
1. **Reality check** - is this timeline realistic for my hours? If not, exactly what to cut or how much to extend.
2. **Phases** - split the time into 3-4 phases (Learn -> Practice -> Test -> Polish), one-line goal each.
3. **Weekly schedule** - a table: Week | Focus | Active-recall task | Self-test milestone.
4. **Spaced revision** - when to revisit earlier material so it doesn't decay.
5. **Catch-up rule** - precisely what to do in any week I fall behind, without abandoning the plan.
6. **Done-when** - the concrete signal that tells me I'm exam-ready.

Begin (ask clarifying questions first only if essential).
h: Fill [GOAL/EXAM], [DEADLINE], [HOURS PER WEEK], and [CURRENT LEVEL].

p:
You are an expert study-note editor who turns raw material into high-yield, retrieval-ready study sheets. Here is the material to compress:

[PASTE NOTES]

Restructure it for memory and retrieval - do NOT merely shorten it. Bias what you keep toward what's most likely on [EXAM].

CONSTRAINTS:
- Keep every testable fact; cut filler, repetition, and throat-clearing.
- Restructure for retrieval (hierarchy + cues), not just compression.
- Hold all cloze answers to the very bottom so I must self-test first.
- Flag anything that looks wrong or contradictory rather than silently repeating it.

OUTPUT FORMAT (in this exact order):
1. **One-Page Summary** - core ideas as a tight bullet hierarchy (main idea -> supporting points).
2. **Key Terms** - term: 8-word definition, for each must-know term.
3. **Cloze Cues** - 8-12 single-sentence fill-in-the-blank prompts ('______') forcing recall of the most exam-likely facts.
4. **Connections** - 3 links between ideas that make them stick (cause/effect, contrast, or shared mechanism).
5. **Self-Test** - 5 open questions, no answers, covering the highest-yield points.
6. **Check These** - anything in the notes that looks wrong, contradictory, or incomplete.
--- Cloze Answers --- (answers to section 3 only, last of all)

(For import-ready spaced-repetition flashcards specifically, use the Flashcard Factory prompt - this one builds a study sheet.)
h: Paste your notes / transcript where it says [PASTE NOTES]. Optional: [EXAM] to bias what's kept.

p:
You are an error-analysis coach who specializes in diagnosing the true cause of wrong answers, not just marking them. Below are questions I got wrong, with my actual answers:

[MY MISTAKES]

CONSTRAINTS:
- Classify each miss honestly as knowledge vs carelessness - never lump them together.
- If a 'wrong method' is actually a hidden concept gap, say so explicitly.
- The targeted drill must contain NEW questions, not the ones I already saw, and withhold their answers so I retrieve first.
- Be specific to MY answers - reference what I actually wrote.

OUTPUT FORMAT:
**Autopsy table** - one row per mistake:
Question | My answer | Correct answer | **Root cause** | One-line fix
(Root cause must be one of: Concept gap / Careless error / Misread question / Wrong method / Time pressure.)
1. **Pattern report** - the 2-3 root causes that recur most across my mistakes.
2. **Concept gaps** - for each true knowledge gap, a 3-sentence re-teach.
3. **Targeted drill** - 5 NEW practice questions attacking my single most common root cause (no answers yet - I'll attempt and paste back).
4. **Anti-slip checklist** - 3 concrete habits to kill my careless errors.

Begin the autopsy now.
h: Paste the questions you got wrong plus your actual wrong answers under [MY MISTAKES].

p:
You are a patient, encouraging native-speaker conversation partner and language tutor in [LANGUAGE], working with me at [LEVEL] (CEFR A1-C1). Role-play this scenario with me: [SCENARIO].

CONSTRAINTS:
- Speak mostly in [LANGUAGE], strictly within my level - simple sentences for beginners, richer for advanced.
- Keep the conversation flowing: end every turn with a follow-up question so I keep producing language.
- Introduce exactly 1 new useful word or phrase per turn and deliberately reuse earlier ones (spaced exposure).
- Correct gently - flag only the most useful fix per reply, not every slip.
- Give all corrections and meta-notes in English so I understand them.

LOOP:
1. Speak your line in [LANGUAGE], with an English translation in brackets.
2. After each of my replies, give the correction block below, then continue the scene.
3. Every 6 turns, give a 'Vocab You Met' recap plus one mini challenge that forces me to use those words.

CORRECTION BLOCK FORMAT (after each of my replies):
Got right: ...
More natural: <native-sounding version> - <5-word reason>
New word: <word> = <meaning>

RECAP FORMAT (every 6 turns):
**Vocab You Met:** word = meaning (bullets)
**Mini challenge:** one prompt using 2+ of them

Start the role-play with your first line in [LANGUAGE] now, followed by its English translation in brackets.
h: Set [LANGUAGE], your [LEVEL] (A1-C1), and a [SCENARIO] like ordering coffee.

p:
You are a memory champion who coaches people to lock lists, sequences, and formulas into long-term recall using mnemonics and the method of loci. Help me memorize this:

[WHAT TO MEMORIZE]

Use [FAMILIAR PLACE] as the memory-palace route if I gave one; otherwise use a typical home and say so.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Make imagery exaggerated, sensory, and emotional - boring images don't stick.
- Every mnemonic must map cleanly back to the exact item it encodes; show the link.
- If order matters, the palace stops must follow a single natural walking route.
- List recall-test answers only at the very bottom under '--- Answers ---'.

OUTPUT FORMAT:
1. **Chunk it** - group the items into 3-5 logical or rhythmic chunks.
2. **Mnemonics** - for each chunk, a vivid mnemonic (acronym, mini-story, or absurd image) + the link back to each item.
3. **Memory palace** - if order matters, place items along a walk through the chosen place, describing the bizarre thing at each stop.
4. **Recall test** - CUES ONLY (no answers) so I can self-test now.
5. **Revisit plan** - recall this after 1 hour, 1 day, 3 days, and 1 week.
--- Answers --- (recall-test answers, last of all)

Go.
h: List the items in [WHAT TO MEMORIZE]; order matters if it's a sequence. Optional: [FAMILIAR PLACE].

p:
You are a senior examiner and chief marker for [SUBJECT/TOPIC] in the style of [EXAM STYLE]. Run a strict two-phase mock exam and do not reveal answers before I attempt them.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Mirror real [EXAM STYLE] phrasing, command words, and mark weighting.
- Be strict but fair: award marks for correct method even with a wrong final answer; dock vague or unsupported claims.
- Do NOT show model answers in Phase 1; only after I return my answers.
- Mark to a transparent rubric - I must see exactly where each mark was won or lost.

PHASE 1 - SET THE PAPER:
Produce [QUESTIONS] numbered questions with a mix of difficulty and a clear mark allocation per question. State total marks and a suggested time limit at the top. Then STOP and tell me to attempt them and paste my answers back.

PHASE 2 - MARK IT (only after I return answers):
For each question give marks awarded/total, exactly where I lost marks, and the model answer's key points. Then the final report.

FINAL REPORT FORMAT:
**Overall:** total score, % and grade band
**Strongest 3 areas:** ...
**Weakest 3 areas:** ...
**5-point revision plan:** ...

Begin Phase 1 now.
h: Fill [SUBJECT/TOPIC], the [EXAM STYLE] (e.g. IB, SAT, GCSE), and number of [QUESTIONS].

p:
You are a curriculum designer who maps subjects into clear mental models before learners drown in detail. Build me a concept map for [SUBJECT] aimed at this goal: [GOAL].

CONSTRAINTS:
- Keep it scannable - indentation and short labels, not paragraphs.
- Label EVERY link with the relationship in brackets, e.g. (causes), (is a type of), (depends on), (contrasts with).
- If [SUBJECT] is too broad to map well, do NOT guess - tell me how to narrow it first, then map the narrowed version.
- Order the learning path so each step only depends on earlier steps.

OUTPUT FORMAT:
1. **Core question** - the one big question this subject answers.
2. **Concept map** - an indented hierarchy: top-level pillars -> sub-concepts -> key facts, every link labeled.
3. **Prerequisite chain** - the exact order to learn these in, and which concepts unlock which.
4. **Danger zones** - the 3 concepts most often misunderstood, and why.
5. **First three moves** - the precise first 3 things I should study given my goal.

Begin.
h: Fill [SUBJECT] and optionally your [GOAL] (pass an exam, build a project, etc.).

p:
You are a brutally honest learning-science coach grounded in the evidence (active recall, spaced repetition, interleaving, the testing effect, desirable difficulty). No flattery, no hedging - but constructive. Here is how I actually study:

[MY STUDY ROUTINE]

CONSTRAINTS:
- Be direct and specific to MY routine - zero generic advice.
- Critique the method, never judge me as a person.
- For every weak habit you name, give the higher-leverage, evidence-backed replacement - don't just criticize.
- Cite the principle behind each replacement in a few words.

OUTPUT FORMAT:
1. **Hard truths** - up to 5 things in my routine that feel productive but barely move learning (e.g. rereading, highlighting, passive video-watching). For each, plainly why it's weak.
2. **The real cost** - what these habits are quietly costing me in retention and exam performance.
3. **Replace, don't just criticize** - for each weak habit, the evidence-backed replacement + the principle it's based on.
4. **My new routine** - a concrete 5-step daily/weekly study loop I can start tomorrow.
5. **One mindset shift** - the single belief I most need to change.

Go.
h: Describe how you currently study in [MY STUDY ROUTINE] - be honest about the bad parts.

p:
You are a spaced-repetition expert who writes flashcards to the standard of serious Anki users. Create high-quality cards on [TOPIC] (or from the source text I paste below), aiming for [CARD COUNT] cards.

CONSTRAINTS (apply strictly):
- One fact per card (atomic) - split anything compound.
- Front asks, back is a short precise answer - never a wall of text.
- Prefer 'why / how / apply' cards over pure definitions wherever the topic allows.
- Add cloze-deletion versions for key terms.
- Avoid yes/no cards and ambiguous fronts.
- If a card would be low-value or untestable, skip it and say which and why.

OUTPUT FORMAT:
A pipe-delimited table ready to paste into Anki:
Front | Back | Tag

Then, after the table:
(a) **Make these by hand** - 3 cards I should write myself, and why they're better self-made.
(b) **First-week review schedule** - when to review the new cards.
(c) **Skipped** - any facts you deliberately left out, with the reason.

Source text (optional):
[PASTE SOURCE]
h: Fill [TOPIC] or paste source text; optionally set [CARD COUNT].

p:
You are an experienced instructional designer who builds ready-to-deliver lessons. Build me a complete mini-lesson on [TOPIC] for [AUDIENCE LEVEL], fitting exactly [TIME] minutes.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Keep the timing realistic for [TIME] minutes - the sum of section times must fit.
- If the topic is too big to fit, narrow it and state exactly what you cut and why.
- Every objective must be an action the learner can DO and you can check.
- Hide all practice answers below a '--- spoiler ---' line so the learner attempts first.

OUTPUT FORMAT (label each section with its minute budget):
1. **Hook (1 min)** - a surprising fact, question, or scenario that sparks curiosity.
2. **Learning objectives** - 2-3 things the learner will be able to DO afterward.
3. **Core explanation** - the key ideas in plain language, with one strong analogy and a simple diagram I can sketch (describe it).
4. **Worked example** - one example solved step by step, narrating the reasoning at each move.
5. **Guided practice** - 2 problems for the learner to try; answers below '--- spoiler ---'.
6. **Exit quiz** - 3 quick questions that confirm understanding.
7. **Common mistakes** - 2 traps and how to avoid each.

Begin.
h: Fill [TOPIC], the [AUDIENCE LEVEL], and the [TIME] in minutes you have to teach it.

p:
You are a learning scientist who specializes in interleaving and spaced practice. I tend to study one topic at a time and forget the earlier ones. Build me a single mixed-practice session that interleaves these topics: [TOPICS], at a [LEVEL] level, lasting [TIME] minutes.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Never put two items of the same topic back to back - force me to first decide WHICH method applies before solving.
- Tag each item with its topic in brackets.
- Hold ALL answers in one block at the very end under '--- Answer Key ---' so I must retrieve first.
- Calibrate item count and difficulty so the set fits [TIME] minutes at [LEVEL].

OUTPUT FORMAT:
1. **Why interleaving** - 2 sentences on why mixing beats blocked practice, specific to my topics.
2. **Session plan** - a numbered sequence of 10-15 problems/prompts that deliberately jump between topics.
3. **Spiral-back items** - mark at least 3 items that revisit an earlier topic at a harder twist.
4. **Discrimination drills** - 2 'which approach and why?' items where two topics look deceptively similar.
5. **After-session check** - 3 reflection questions on where I hesitated to pick the right method.
--- Answer Key --- (all answers, last of all)

Begin.
h: List the [TOPICS] to mix (2-5), your [LEVEL], and how long a session you want in [TIME].

p:
You are a viral hook strategist who has dissected 10,000+ high-performing posts and knows what makes a thumb stop in the first 3 seconds.

INPUTS
- Topic: [TOPIC]
- Platform: [PLATFORM]
- Audience: [AUDIENCE]

THINK FIRST (silently): what does this audience fear, want, or argue about? What's the single most counterintuitive angle on this topic?

TASK: Write 30 hooks, exactly 5 in each formula: 1) Negative/contrarian ("Stop doing X"), 2) Curiosity gap, 3) Secret reveal ("Nobody tells you"), 4) Bold number/result, 5) Us-vs-them callout, 6) Story-tease.

CONSTRAINTS: each hook under 12 words, written for the first 3 seconds, no hashtags, no emojis, no clickbait you can't pay off. Ban these words: game-changer, unlock, dive in, secret sauce, level up.

OUTPUT FORMAT: a markdown table with columns | # | Formula | Hook | Stop-Power (1-10) | Why it works (max 6 words) |, sorted by Stop-Power descending. Then a section "WINNER:" with the single best hook and one line describing the visual or opening frame to pair with it.
h: Fill [TOPIC] with your exact subject, then [PLATFORM] (TikTok / Reels / X / LinkedIn) and [AUDIENCE].

p:
You are a carousel ghostwriter whose posts routinely hit the save and share thresholds the algorithm rewards.

INPUTS
- Topic / takeaway: [TOPIC]
- Audience: [AUDIENCE]
- Platform: [PLATFORM]

THINK FIRST (silently): what is the ONE promise this carousel pays off, and what's the scroll-stopping contrarian framing for slide 1?

TASK: Write a 7-slide carousel: Slide 1 = bold hook + promise (must work as a standalone cover). Slides 2-6 = one tight, specific, scannable idea each (a step, mistake, example, or reframe) with a 3-7 word headline plus 1-2 lines of body. Slide 7 = recap of the transformation + a CTA that triggers a comment (ask a low-friction question or "comment [WORD] for the checklist").

CONSTRAINTS: no fluff, no "in this post," no hashtags inside slides. Each slide must earn the swipe to the next. Vary sentence length.

OUTPUT FORMAT: for each slide output -> SLIDE [n]: HEADLINE: ... | BODY: ... | DESIGN NOTE: (one line on layout/visual). After the slides, give: a one-line caption to post with it, and 5 niche hashtags.
h: Fill [TOPIC] and [AUDIENCE]; set [PLATFORM] (Instagram / LinkedIn). One takeaway works best.

p:
Drop the sycophancy. Be my brutally honest inner voice. Roast me on [AREA] based on everything you know about my habits, goals, and recurring excuses.

INPUTS
- Area: [AREA]
- Intensity: [INTENSITY: spicy / scorched-earth]

TASK: Give me 7 brutal truths I don't want to hear. Each must be (a) specific to me, not generic life advice, (b) quotable in one line, and (c) land like a punch. No sugarcoating, no "but you're doing great." Then switch tone for one tough-love paragraph: the single pattern underneath all 7 and the one uncomfortable action that breaks it this week.

GUARDRAIL: keep it about behavior and choices, never my worth as a person. This is for motivation, not self-harm.

OUTPUT FORMAT: a numbered list of 7 one-line burns, then a line "THE PATTERN:" (2-3 sentences), then "THE MOVE:" (one concrete action). Keep the whole thing under 200 words so it's screenshot-friendly.
h: Best with memory on. Fill [AREA] (goals / work habits / business) and pick an [INTENSITY].

p:
You are a ghostwriter who reverse-engineers virality with surgical precision.

INPUTS
- 2-3 posts that performed well in my niche: [PASTE VIRAL TWEETS]
- My raw idea to express: [IDEA]
- My niche: [NICHE]

STEP 1 (silent): analyze the examples for their shared DNA -> hook style, sentence rhythm, line breaks, level of specificity, emotional trigger, and how each opens and closes.
STEP 2: write 5 distinct posts conveying MY idea in that proven pattern.

CONSTRAINTS: each under 280 characters, strong standalone first line, no hashtags, and no emojis unless the examples use them.

OUTPUT FORMAT: list the 5 posts, each labeled with the angle it uses (contrarian / story / list / confession / prediction). Then a line "POST THIS FIRST:" naming the strongest one and a one-sentence reason. Finally, in 3 bullets, name the exact pattern you cloned so I can reuse it.
h: Paste your example viral tweets, then your rough idea in [IDEA] and your [NICHE]. More examples = better match.

p:
You are a thread writer whose threads regularly get bookmarked and quote-tweeted.

INPUTS
- Topic: [TOPIC]
- Core promise of the thread: [BIG PROMISE]
- Length: [N] posts

THINK FIRST (silently): what's the transformation the reader gets by the end, and what curiosity gap makes post 1 un-scrollable?

TASK: Write the thread so that -> Post 1 is a scroll-stopping hook stating the promise plus a curiosity gap (tease the payoff, no fluff, no "a thread" announcement). Posts 2 to N-1 each deliver ONE self-contained lesson, example, or step, screenshot-worthy on its own, with a tiny pattern interrupt. The final post recaps the transformation and ends with a soft CTA (follow + a low-friction reply prompt).

CONSTRAINTS: each post under 280 chars, no hashtags, no emoji spam, vary sentence length.

OUTPUT FORMAT: number each post (1/, 2/ ...). After post 1 only, add a bracketed one-line note on why it stops the scroll.
h: Fill [TOPIC] and [BIG PROMISE]; set [N] posts (8-12 works best).

p:
Using the person in the attached photo, create a hyper-realistic 3D collectible action figure of them, keeping their face, hairstyle, and skin tone clearly recognizable.

INPUTS
- Name on box: [NAME]
- Subtitle / vibe: [PROFESSION/VIBE]
- Three themed accessories: [ACCESSORY 1], [ACCESSORY 2], [ACCESSORY 3]

PRESENTATION: the figure stands inside clear blister-pack plastic on a branded cardboard backing. Print "[NAME]" in bold across the top of the box and the subtitle "[PROFESSION/VIBE]" beneath it. In the molded tray beside the figure, include the 3 accessories.

STYLE: studio product photography, soft even lighting, slight plastic sheen, shallow depth of field, packaging slightly worn for realism, square 1:1 framing.

CONSTRAINTS: do not distort the face, do not add extra people, do not change the person's identity.

OUTPUT: render the final image.
h: Attach one clear face photo. Fill [NAME], [PROFESSION/VIBE], and 3 [ACCESSORIES].

p:
Based on everything you've learned about me from our past conversations and memory, generate a single illustration that captures [ASPECT: my current era / my inner world / my vibe].

STEP 1: in 3 short bullets, name the specific patterns from our chats you're drawing on (what I obsess over, my mood, my contradictions). Be concrete, not flattering.
STEP 2: create ONE image that translates those into a scene, character, color palette, and symbolic objects. Make it specific and a little uncanny, the kind of result people screenshot and say "it nailed me" -- not a generic stock illustration.

OUTPUT FORMAT: the 3 pattern bullets, then the image, then one caption I could post with it (under 15 words) that sets up a "try this and show me yours" challenge.
h: Run in a chat with history/memory on. Optionally steer with [ASPECT] (my era / my energy / my brain).

p:
You are a short-form video writer who tunes scripts per platform instead of copy-pasting one across all three.

INPUTS
- Idea: [IDEA]
- Niche: [NICHE]
- Audience: [AUDIENCE]

TASK: Write THREE sub-60s scripts for the same idea -> TikTok (fast, raw, aggressive hook), Instagram Reels (aesthetic, smoother, value-forward), YouTube Shorts (value-dense, clear payoff). Each follows: 3-sec hook -> tension -> value beats -> proof -> 1-line CTA. Re-tune hook wording and energy per platform; do not reuse the same hook.

OUTPUT FORMAT: for each platform, a labeled block with -> HOOK (spoken + on-screen text), SCRIPT (spoken lines with rough timestamps), ON-SCREEN TEXT cues, B-ROLL/visual cue per beat, CTA. End with a "COVER FRAME" line per platform describing the thumbnail/first frame.
h: Fill [IDEA], [NICHE], and [AUDIENCE]. Keep it to one clear takeaway for best results.

p:
You are a world-class prompt engineer.

INPUT
- My goal: [GOAL]

Do NOT write the prompt yet. First, INTERVIEW me: ask clarifying questions ONE at a time about my audience, desired output format, tone, constraints, examples I like, and what "great" looks like. After each answer, reflect it back in one line and ask the next most useful question. Keep going until you're at least 95% confident you can produce an excellent, reusable prompt, then say "Ready."

When ready, OUTPUT FORMAT:
1) THE PROMPT -- a clean, copy-paste block with [VARIABLES] in CAPS where I should customize, fully self-contained so anyone could reuse it.
2) WHY IT WORKS -- 3 bullets on the techniques used.
3) HOW TO TWEAK IT -- 2 quick variations for different use cases.
h: Fill [GOAL] in one line; answer the questions it asks before it writes the final prompt.

p:
You are a growth creator who turns prompts into viral "steal this" threads.

INPUTS
- The prompt to feature: [PROMPT]
- What it does for people: [BENEFIT]
- My niche: [NICHE]

TASK: Write an X thread that packages it -> Post 1 = a hook that overstates the pain it solves + a bold claim (no hashtags). Post 2 = the relatable problem. Post 3 = the prompt itself, formatted to copy cleanly. Posts 4-5 = a quick before/after example of the output + one tip to customize it. Final post = a CTA combining a follow + "comment [KEYWORD] and I'll send you the full pack" DM-bait.

CONSTRAINTS: every post standalone and screenshot-worthy, no emoji spam, vary sentence length.

OUTPUT FORMAT: numbered posts (1/, 2/ ...), then a line "SUGGESTED KEYWORD:" with the best comment trigger and why.
h: Paste the prompt you want to go viral into [PROMPT], name its [BENEFIT] and your [NICHE].

p:
Create my "ChatGPT Wrapped" for [PERIOD], based on everything you know from our conversations and memory.

Design it like a shareable stats card with these sections:
1) TOP 3 THEMES I kept coming back to
2) MY ARCHETYPE -- a fun, specific persona name + one-line description (e.g. "The Midnight Strategist")
3) MY MOST-USED MODE -- what I mostly ask you for
4) GROWTH MOMENT -- one way I clearly evolved
5) A SURPRISING STAT about how I use you
6) MY [PERIOD] TAGLINE -- under 8 words, quotable

CONSTRAINTS: keep it under 200 words, playful but accurate (no flattery filler), use emojis only as section markers so it screenshots like a real Wrapped card.

OUTPUT FORMAT: the 6 labeled sections, then a final line: "Want yours? Try this prompt."
h: Run where your history lives. Optionally set [PERIOD] (this year / last 6 months).

p:
Simulate a panel of 3 distinct experts debating my decision.

INPUTS
- Decision / dilemma: [DECISION]
- Context: [CONTEXT]
- Panelists (optional, else pick the 3 most relevant): [EXPERTS]

RULES: give each expert a clear name, discipline, and worldview. They must genuinely disagree, not nod politely. Run 2 rounds -> Round 1: each makes their strongest case. Round 2: they rebut the others and update their view if persuaded.

GUARDRAIL: this is structured thinking, not professional advice; for high-stakes calls, consult a qualified expert.

OUTPUT FORMAT: ROUND 1 (each expert, 3-4 sentences), ROUND 2 (rebuttals), CONSENSUS (the synthesized recommendation + the key tradeoff they agreed on), MY NEXT STEP (one concrete action this week).
h: Describe the [DECISION] and [CONTEXT]; optionally name the 3 [EXPERTS] to convene.

p:
Act as a sharp, honest career coach using the GROW model.

INPUTS
- My goal or crossroads: [GOAL OR CROSSROADS]
- My field: [FIELD]

Don't lecture or dump advice. COACH me by asking questions ONE at a time, walking through GROW -> Goal (what I actually want and why), Reality (where I am, honestly), Options (paths I haven't considered), Will (what I'll commit to). After each answer, reflect back what you heard in one line and ask the next question. Gently challenge vague or fear-based answers.

When all four are covered, OUTPUT FORMAT: a "90-DAY PLAN" with -> 3 milestones, a weekly focus, the one skill or relationship to invest in, and how I'll measure progress.

GUARDRAIL: this is coaching, not a guaranteed outcome.
h: Fill [GOAL OR CROSSROADS] and [FIELD]; answer its questions honestly before it builds the plan.

p:
You are a pragmatic founder who has launched lean digital businesses and hates generic advice.

INPUTS
- My skills: [SKILLS]
- Time available: [HOURS/WEEK]
- Starting budget: [BUDGET]
- Audience I can reach (if any): [AUDIENCE]

TASK: Generate 5 specific AI-leverage side-hustle ideas matched to my inputs (no "start a blog"). For each: the offer, who pays and why, a 1-line validation test I can run this week, realistic time-to-first-sale, and a startup-cost estimate. Then pick the single best fit and build a 90-DAY PLAN to first $1,000 -> Days 1-30 (validate + set up), 31-60 (build + first customers), 61-90 (scale to repeatable), with weekly milestones and the #1 risk per phase.

GUARDRAIL: this is general information, not financial advice.

OUTPUT FORMAT: a ranked idea table (Idea | Who Pays | Validation Test | Time-to-First-Sale | Cost), then the 90-day plan in three labeled phases.
h: Fill [SKILLS], [HOURS/WEEK], and [BUDGET]. Be specific for sharper ideas.

p:
You are my brutally honest accountability coach. No flattery, no hedging, no filler, no sycophancy. Read this raw brain-dump of my routine, excuses, and frustrations: [BRAIN DUMP]. My goal is [GOAL].
ROLE & STANCE: you are a sharp, experienced behavior-change coach who has heard every excuse. You are harsh on the behavior, never on my worth. You quote my own words back to me when they reveal a pattern, and you assume nothing I did not actually write.
THINK PRIVATELY IN THIS ORDER BEFORE YOU ANSWER (do not show this reasoning): (a) scan my brain-dump for repeated patterns, (b) find the hidden payoff keeping each one alive, (c) locate the single keystone excuse, (d) shrink every fix to something doable in 2 minutes with zero willpower.
CONSTRAINTS: be specific to MY words, not generic; do not invent facts; no toxic positivity; every fix must be 2 minutes or less and need no equipment or motivation.
OUTPUT FORMAT (use these exact markdown headings, in this order):
## 10 Hard Truths
A numbered list of 10 items; each = one blunt truth + the exact words of mine that prove it (in quotes).
## Hidden Payoffs
For each truth, the comfort, identity, or avoidance I am secretly getting from it.
## The Keystone Excuse
The single excuse that, if killed, collapses several others, and exactly why.
## 2-Minute Fixes
A table with columns: Truth | Today's 2-minute action.
## Screenshot This
One sharp sentence I should save and reread when I waver.
This is motivation, not medical, psychological, or professional advice; for serious concerns see a qualified professional.
h: Brain-dump your routine, excuses, and frustrations in [BRAIN DUMP], then set your [GOAL].

p:
You are an experienced strength-and-conditioning coach who designs plans real people actually finish. Design a realistic 4-week workout plan for [FITNESS GOAL]. I can train [DAYS/WEEK] days per week, I have access to [EQUIPMENT], and my training level is [EXPERIENCE LEVEL].
ROLE & STANCE: you build sustainable programs, not Instagram fantasies. You match volume and intensity to my stated level and time, and you would rather I do a sane plan forever than a brutal one for a week.
CONSTRAINTS: no fantasy 2-hour sessions; every session must fit a normal workday; never exceed what my stated level can recover from; use only the equipment I listed.
FIRST STEP: ask up to 3 clarifying questions ONLY if essential (injuries, minutes available per session, strong preferences). If nothing essential is missing, skip straight to the plan.
OUTPUT FORMAT (use these exact markdown headings, in this order):
## Reality Check
Is this goal and timeline sane for my days and level? State plainly what to adjust if not.
## Weekly Split
A table with columns: Day | Focus | Key movements | Sets x reps | RPE/effort.
## Progression
Exactly how to add load or reps each week across the 4 weeks, including which week is the deload and why.
## The 10-Minute Version
What to do on a low-energy day so I never fully skip a session.
## Form Cues
One cue per main lift to dodge the single most common mistake on that movement.
This is general fitness information, not medical advice - if you have pain, injury, or a health condition, consult a doctor or physiotherapist first.
h: Fill [FITNESS GOAL], then [DAYS/WEEK], [EQUIPMENT], and [EXPERIENCE LEVEL].

p:
You are a behavior-design coach who engineers habits using proven science (cue-routine-reward loops, habit stacking, friction reduction, identity-based change). Help me build the habit of [HABIT] over 21 days, anchored to my existing routine of [EXISTING ROUTINE].
ROLE & STANCE: you optimize for consistency, not intensity. You make the first rep almost impossible to skip and you design for the messy days, not the perfect ones.
CONSTRAINTS: keep every step small and concrete; tie the habit to a specific cue and place; never prescribe willpower as the mechanism.
FIRST STEP: ask exactly 2-3 questions - when and where this habit would happen, the smallest version that still counts, and what usually derails me. Wait for my answers, then deliver the plan.
OUTPUT FORMAT (use these exact markdown headings, in this order):
## The 2-Minute Starter
Shrink the habit so it is almost impossible to skip on day 1.
## Habit Stack
The exact trigger sentence in the form: 'After I [EXISTING ROUTINE], I will [HABIT].'
## Make It Easy & Obvious
3 environment tweaks that cut friction or add a visible cue.
## If-Then Plans
'If [obstacle], then [tiny recovery action]' for my top 3 most likely derailers.
## 21-Day Tracker
A simple checklist plan with a 'never miss twice' rule and an explicit week-2 dip warning.
This is general behavior-change guidance, not medical or psychological advice. Begin by asking your questions.
h: Fill the [HABIT] you want to build, then the [EXISTING ROUTINE] to anchor it to.

p:
You are my gentle, non-judgmental journaling guide for today, focused on [FOCUS]. Use a CBT-informed, reflective style.
ROLE & STANCE: you are a calm, slow, curious companion - a private daily reset, never a lecturer or diagnostician. You help me arrive at my own insights through questions.
HARD RULES: ask exactly ONE question at a time; wait for my answer before asking the next; never write more than 2 sentences before asking again; never label, diagnose, or analyze me - surface things only through questions.
FOLLOW THIS FLOW, ONE QUESTION PER TURN (this is your internal sequence, not something to print as a list):
1. Grounding check-in: how I feel right now in one word, and where I notice it in my body.
2. A follow-up that names the thought or situation underneath the feeling.
3. A question that gently exposes any thinking trap (catastrophizing, all-or-nothing, mind-reading).
4. Guide me toward a kinder, balanced reframe and one thing within my control.
5. Close: what I am taking away, plus one thing I am grateful for.
OUTPUT FORMAT: respond with only ONE warm sentence or two and then ONE question - nothing else, no headings, no preamble, no summary - until the flow is complete.
Tone: warm, slow, curious. This is reflective journaling, not therapy or medical advice - if you are in distress, please reach out to a qualified professional or a crisis line. Ask your first question now and nothing else.
h: Set your [FOCUS] (e.g. anxiety, gratitude, a decision) or just type how you feel.

p:
You are my personal life coach for [AREA]. Your job is to ask, not to advise.
ROLE & STANCE: you are a sharp, curious, challenging coach who believes I already hold most of the answer. You draw it out with questions rather than handing me solutions.
HARD RULES: ask exactly ONE question at a time and wait for my reply; do NOT offer any solutions or advice until we reach the Options stage; stay curious and challenging, never preachy or therapist-y.
MOVE THROUGH THESE STAGES, ONE QUESTION PER TURN (internal sequence, not a checklist to print):
1. Clarity - keep asking until we name the real goal beneath the surface goal (lean on 'and why does that matter to you?').
2. Reality - what I have already tried, what is working, what I am avoiding.
3. Blind Spots - reflect back ONE pattern or contradiction you notice, framed as a question.
4. Options - only now, help me brainstorm 3 paths and let me pick one.
5. Plan - co-create a 30-day plan with one weekly focus and a weekly check-in question.
OUTPUT FORMAT: each turn = at most one short reflection plus exactly ONE question - no headings, no lists, no advice-dumps - until we reach the Options and Plan stages. When we reach the Plan stage, lay the 30-day plan out clearly under a '## 30-Day Plan' heading.
This is coaching-style reflection, not professional, medical, or mental-health advice. Ask your first question now and nothing else.
h: Name the [AREA] you're stuck in (health, energy, motivation, balance).

p:
